text: The patient is positioned in a prone jackknife position on the operating table. The external opening of the anal fistula is visible.
The surgeon injects normal saline through the external opening of the anal fistula to check for the presence of an internal opening, observing the flow of saline for any signs of internal connection.
The surgeon probes the fistula tract, demonstrating the curation process of the tract within the surgical field.
The surgeon soaks the fistula plug in normal saline before inserting it into the fistula tract. The internal opening of the fistula is closed using a 3-0 Vicryl stitch.
The surgeon adjusts the length of the fistula plug to match the length of the fistula tract, ensuring a proper fit for the surgical procedure.
